reasoning: Attacked at the microcode level and it held. I decoded the Math Box rather than taking POSITH at face value. MBUDOC.DOC:27-69 gives the register map, and RBARON.MAC:593-604 names it in the ROM's own words: MM.AL/AM (MATH+0/1) = COSINE, MM.BL/BM (MATH+2/3) = SINE, MM.EL/FL (MATH+4/6) = X/Y TRANSLATE, MM.XL/YL (MATH+8/0A) = the point. Writing MM.YM (MATH+0B) starts the multiply -> output = (x-e)*a - (y-f)*b; writing M.SYM (MATH+12) -> output = (x-e)*b + (y-f)*a. That is ONE 2x2 rotation and it is the only rotation the hardware offers. PFTRIG (RBARON.MAC:5981-5982, 'LDA PFROTN+1 / LDY PFROTN') loads that angle from PFROTN, which PFMOTN sets to PLDELX x 8 (RBARON.MAC:3214-3224) -- the BANK angle. So the single rotation is a roll about the view axis: the auditor's 'single Z rotation' is exactly right. There is provably NO pitch: an X-axis rotation would have to mix Y with depth, and POSITH never does -- it copies OBJECT+4/5 into OBJECT+6/7 verbatim (RBGRND.MAC:269-272) and feeds both raw into the divisor register X' (MATH+15/16, MBUDOC 'XPL/XPH'). Climb/dive enters ONLY as the SBC I4YPOS / SBC I4YPOS+1 borrow chain (RBGRND.MAC:277-283), a vertical eye TRANSLATION applied before the divide-by-depth. The ROM corroborates this in its own vocabulary: RBARON.MAC:1244 comments 'STA I4YPOS ;INITIALIZE HORIZON Y POSITION', and I4YPOS's clamps are named PLYMIN/PLYMAX = 'PF HORIZON LINE MIN/MAX Y' (RBARON.MAC:446-447, applied to I4YPOS at 3253-3266). Eye altitude IS what moves the picture. Radix checked: all cited RBARON lines sit in the hex region opened at :74 (the only decimal island is 6217-6280). Our side verified independently: flight.ts:97 PITCH_SCALE = Math.PI/512 and flight.ts:196 'pitch: state.pitchRate * PITCH_SCALE'; PITCH_TABLE's min is -32 (flight.ts:44) so -32 * pi/512 = -pi/16 = -0.19635 rad = -11.25 deg exactly as claimed; pitchRate is documented as 'this frame's climb/dive step (transient; 0 = level)' (flight.ts:119-120), so the tilt does snap back when the stick centres. My own grep confirms toEye is referenced nowhere in src/ -- only its definition at flight.ts:205 and tests/. main.ts:191 does pass [0,0,0]. The mechanism is real, the arithmetic is exact, and the divergence is substantive: an eye translation gives depth-divided parallax (distant things barely move, near ground rushes) while a camera rotationX gives a uniform angular shift -- they are not interchangeable, so this cannot be waved through as a re-parameterisation.
corrections: SCOPE UNDERSTATED: the finding names two eye-at-origin sites; there are THREE. It missed main.ts:185, 'mountainSegments(mountains, attitude, [0, 0, 0], aspect)' -- the SCAPE mountains are also pinned to the origin. This makes the 'altitude has ZERO effect on the picture' claim MORE true than stated, not less.

GOOD NEWS FOR THE FIX the auditor did not spot: landscape.ts already accepts and threads an eye (landscape.ts:104-112, 'flightView(attitude, eye)'), so part (b) needs no signature change there -- only the caller at main.ts:185. The three sites to fix are main.ts:185, main.ts:191, and horizon.ts:31/35 (EYE_AT_ORIGIN).

Fix framing: do NOT replace the camera pitch with a different camera ROTATION. The ROM's altitude term is divided by depth, so it is a translation of the eye, not an orientation change. Part (b) (pass toEye(flight)) is the whole of the real effect; part (a) is pure removal.
This finding and FL-017 share the same fix (thread toEye into flightView). Land them together.

reasoning: Verified in all three directions the brief demands and it survived. (1) RADIX: RBARON.MAC opens '.RADIX 16' at :74 and the only decimal island in the file is 6217-6280 (back to hex at :6281). Line 456 is therefore hex: HORIZN = 0x40 = 64. This is independently corroborated from inside the same equate block, where the author writes trailing-dot decimals precisely because the region is hex -- PF.QTX = 512.*4 (:452), PF.SGX = 256.*4 (:453), L.OBJ = 28. (:462). A decimal reading of :456 is impossible. (2) UNCONDITIONAL: I read the add end to end. RBGRND.MAC:301-307 is 'CLC / LDA YLOW / ADC I,HORIZN / STA XPOS+2 / LDA YHIGH / ADC I,0 / STA XPOS+3' -- a straight 16-bit add with no branch guarding it, applied to the quotient AFTER the divide-by-depth (the divide is started at :299, MATH+14 = SZXD). POSITP (RBGRND.MAC:261-267) sets the translate registers and falls STRAIGHT THROUGH into POSITH, so it inherits the offset too. Every call site is commented by the author himself as 'ADD HORIZN ADJ' (RBARON.MAC:3736, 4423, 4445, 4488, 4538). Note it is added to Y only, never to X (the X result at RBGRND.MAC:312-321 has no such add) -- so it is a pure vertical bias, as claimed. (3) INDEPENDENT CORROBORATION the auditor did not find: RBARON.MAC:2747-2754 (PLNDEL) does 'SEC / LDA ZX,PLSTAT+8 / SBC I,HORIZN / STA XPOS' -- code that needs a true, unbiased Y must SUBTRACT HORIZN back out. That only makes sense if the offset is baked into every projected Y. (4) OURS: I grepped rather than trusting the auditor. sceneProjection is 'perspective(VERTICAL_FOV, aspect, NEAR, FAR)' (scene.ts:36-37) -- a symmetric frustum with no principal-point term -- and there is no HORIZN, no screen-Y bias, and no off-centre projection anywhere in src/. horizon.ts:17-18 still carries the deferral comment. We genuinely lack it.
corrections: TITLE/CLAIM WORDING IS WRONG AND WOULD MISLEAD THE FIX: 'equivalently the camera looks 64 units down' is false. A constant added AFTER the perspective divide is depth-INDEPENDENT; a camera pitch produces a depth-DEPENDENT shift. HORIZN is a principal-point / lens-shift offset, not a rotation. The fix is an off-centre projection matrix (a y-offset in sceneProjection), NOT a rotationX. The auditor's own reasoning text gets this right ('constant, global, positive Y bias applied after the divide-by-depth') -- only the headline is wrong. Given FL-014 is about deleting a fabricated camera pitch, shipping this title as written risks re-introducing one.

SIZE, which the auditor declined to give: it can be bounded WITHOUT decoding the Math Box, because the ROM's own cull tests are in the same XPOS units as HORIZN. SETGRS (RBGRND.MAC:345-355) culls at |X| >= 0x220 (544) and |Y| >= 0x188 (392); 544/392 = 1.39, i.e. essentially the 4:3 screen. So HORIZN = 64 is about 16% of the screen HALF-height, i.e. roughly 8% of full screen height. Real and visible, but a framing nudge -- not the dramatic shift the title's 'lifts the whole scene' might suggest.

SCOPE: 'EVERY projected point' means every point placed through POSITH/POSITP -- the 3D scene (horizon, PF/SCAPE objects, planes, shells). The cockpit, crosshair, score and alphanumerics do not go through POSITH and must NOT receive the offset.

reasoning: Held on mechanism, but both of its numbers are wrong -- see corrections. RADIX: RBARON.MAC:451 sits in the hex region (opened :74, only decimal island 6217-6280), so HORZ = 0x1000 = 4096. VERIFIED. I then attacked the operand the auditor could have fumbled: 'LDA I,HORZ/100' (:4393). The 100 is HEX = 256, so HORZ/100 = 0x1000/0x100 = 0x10, stored to OBJECT+5 (the depth MSB) while OBJECT+4 (LSB) was zeroed by the LDY I,0 at :4387 -- reassembling to a 16-bit depth of exactly 0x1000 = 4096. The auditor's reading survives the trap. SCENE (RBARON.MAC:4387-4394) does set world Y = 0 (STY OBJECT+2 / STY OBJECT+3), sets X at :4402-4403, and calls POSITH at :4423 -- which subtracts I4YPOS (RBGRND.MAC:277-283) before the divide. So the ROM horizon IS at finite depth and DOES move with altitude. DECISIVE CORROBORATION THE AUDITOR MISSED: the ROM says so itself. RBARON.MAC:1244 comments 'STA I4YPOS ;INITIALIZE HORIZON Y POSITION', and the clamps applied to I4YPOS at :3253-3266 are named PLYMIN/PLYMAX = 'PF HORIZON LINE MIN Y' / 'MAX Y' (RBARON.MAC:446-447). In the ROM's own vocabulary the eye altitude IS the horizon line's screen Y. Our horizon.ts:8-9 header asserts the exact opposite and hard-codes EYE_AT_ORIGIN (horizon.ts:31/35). ON THE TEAM LEAD'S QUESTION -- is 'horizon at infinity' a defensible rendering equivalent? NO. The ROM's horizon is not a true horizon at all: it is a line at GROUND level (world Y=0) at FINITE depth 4096. Drawing it at infinity turns it into a true vanishing line, which BY CONSTRUCTION cannot move with eye height. So our version is more physically correct and less faithful, and it discards the only cue the ROM uses to show altitude -- in a game whose altitude is clamped, crashable, and whose clamps are literally named after the horizon line. That is a genuine visual/gameplay divergence, not a scaling nuance.
corrections: MAGNITUDE BADLY UNDERSTATED. 'it DOES move as the player climbs and dives, just slowly' is wrong -- the effect is LARGE. Using the ROM's own numbers: screen_Y_horizon = -I4YPOS*k/HORZ + HORIZN. The I4YPOS clamps are PLYMIN=0x8*4=32 and PLYMAX=0x180*4=1536, and the screen half-height is ~392 XPOS units (from SETGRS, RBGRND.MAC:353). Those fit a net gain k/HORZ ~= 1/4 -- which is also dimensionally right, since ROM coords are stored x4 (UNIV4X = 'UNIVERSE X (* 4)', I4YPOS = 'PLAYER Y POSITION * 4'). That gives screen Y ~= -I4YPOS/4 + 64: about +56 at minimum altitude and about -320 at maximum -- a ~376-unit sweep across a +/-392 half-screen. The horizon traverses very nearly the WHOLE screen between the altitude clamps. CAVEAT: I derived the 1/4 gain by fitting the ROM's own clamp names and cull box, not by decoding the Math Box sine-table scale, so treat the exact constant as approximate -- but the ORDER is certainly 'most of the screen', not 'slight'.

THE PROPOSED FIX CONSTANT IS WRONG BY 4x. 'set HORIZON_DISTANCE = 0x1000' conflates unit systems. ROM depths are stored x4 (RBGRND.MAC:488-496 label the X/Y/Z operands '(*4)'), and our own bridge already divides by 4 (flight.ts:101, ALT_TO_Y = 1/4). So HORZ = 4096 ROM units = 1024 WORLD units in our space. HORIZON_DISTANCE should become 1024, not 4096 -- setting 4096 would put the horizon 4x too far out and quarter the altitude coupling, silently reproducing most of the bug.

Size 's' is right for the edit, but it is coupled to FL-014(b): both require threading toEye(flight) into flightView. Land them as one change or the horizon and the scene will disagree about where the eye is.

reasoning: KILLED. The finding's central claim is that 'MAIN skips PFMOTN when the game is frozen or the player has hit the ground while POTSCL still runs'. Both halves are false, and the two gates that kill them sit DIRECTLY ABOVE the lines the auditor cited. They quoted MAIN from :780 and never read :774-777. MAIN's actual gate chain (RBARON.MAC:774-785) is: (774) 'LDA GMEND1 / BMI 10$ ;END OF LIFE' -> skip POTSCL entirely; (776) 'LDA GAMODE / BPL 5$ ;ONLY IN PLAY' -> skip POTSCL unless GAMODE.D7 set; (778) JSR POTSCL; (781) 'LSR / BCS 20$ ;FREEZE' -> skip PFMOTN on GAMODE.D0; (783) 'BIT GREND / BVS 20$' -> skip PFMOTN on GREND.D6; (785) JSR PFMOTN. So POTSCL runs iff GMEND1.D7==0 AND GAMODE.D7==1. FREEZE HALF: GAMODE.D0 is a FREEZE bit -- the ROM says so at RBARON.MAC:980, 'ORA I,0B ;D3 SET (AND FREEZE BITS)' -- and every store that sets it also CLEARS D7: :1914-1915 'LDA I,3 / STA GAMODE ;ATTRACT', :1930-1931 'LDA I,7 / STA GAMODE ;GET HIGH SCORE INITIALS', :973-975 'AND I,33 ;FREEZE, DISPLAY STATUS / ORA I,.STRTP / STA GAMODE', and :1683-1685 ORs 3 onto a GAMODE already known to have D7==0 (reached only via 'AND I,0BF / BEQ' at :1663-1665). GAMODE.D7 ('in play', cf. :1232 'BMI INITIAL ;GAME') and GAMODE.D0 (freeze) are therefore MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE, and POTSCL is gated on D7. POTSCL DOES NOT RUN WHEN THE GAME IS FROZEN. GROUND HALF: GREND has exactly two writers. :3758-3759 'LDA I,80 / STA GREND ;SHELL CD' sets D7 with D6 CLEAR -- so it does not trip the :783 BVS at all. :4643-4645 'LDA SAVX+1 / ORA I,0C0 / STA GREND ;D6=GROUND COLLISION' is the ONLY writer that sets D6, and it ALWAYS ORs in 0xC0, so D6==1 implies D7==1. And GREND.D7 forces EOL in the SAME FRAME: GREND is set at :4645 inside SCENE (called from MAIN:810), and EOLSEQ is called fifteen lines later at MAIN:825, where 'BIT GREND / BPL 10$' (:1059) sees D7 set and falls through to 'STX GMEND1' (:1067) with X = 0xC0 or 0xA0 -- both D7 set. On the very next MAIN iteration, :774's 'BMI 10$' skips POTSCL. So there is NEVER a frame on which GREND.D6 causes PFMOTN to be skipped while POTSCL still runs -- ZERO frames, not 'the whole crash'. NET: on every frame POTSCL actually executes, PFMOTN executes right after it and unconditionally overwrites PFROTN with PLDELX x 8 (RBARON.MAC:3214-3224, three ASLs, STA PFROTN with no branch), and nothing reads PFROTN in between (PFTRIG is at MAIN:801, after PFMOTN). POTSCL's return-to-centre block is dead in the shipped ROM -- its result is always overwritten before anything can see it. The horizon never 'rights itself at 2 counts per frame' in any state. What the ROM ACTUALLY does on a crash is a dedicated EOL animation: EOLSEQ zeroes PLDELX/PLDELY outright ('NO PLAYER MOTION', :1111-1113) and sets GMEND1.D6 = 'SCROLL HORIZON, ROTATE PLAYFIELD' (:1120-1122) -- a separate mechanic, not POTSCL decay. The auditor's own reasoning warns the block 'is easy to mistake for the main bank law'; they then made the inverse error, granting it a reachable state it does not have. Accepting this would have invited someone to build a freeze/crash horizon-decay path the ROM never had. (For the record, the arithmetic that IS right: PFROTN's circle is 2048 counts -- TRIG at :5984-5990 shifts the 16-bit PFROTN right by 2 into a 7-bit angle + 2-bit quadrant -- so 1 count = 360/2048 = 0.176 deg and the 0x100 clamp = 45 deg, which matches our ROLL_SCALE = pi/1024. That part of our code is correct and needs no change.)
corrections: Also wrong on its own terms even if it had been reachable: '(step -2, or -1 at |1|)' misreads the test. POTSCL loads only the LOW BYTE (:5876 'LDA PFROTN') and tests 'LSR / BNE', so the -1 step fires whenever PFROTN's low byte is 0 or 1 -- which includes PFROTN = +0x100, the saturated full-bank value, not just |PFROTN| == 1. The negative branch (:5882-5886) is likewise a low-byte-only test against 0xFF. It is an 8-bit test on a 16-bit quantity.

Recommendation stands as 'accept', but for the OPPOSITE reason to the one given: not 'low consequence', but 'no consequence' -- there is no ROM behaviour here to miss. Do not implement a freeze/crash horizon-decay path.
